LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. AND KEVIN WALLACE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH RULE OF COURT 3.1322 ("A NOTICE OF MOTION TO STRIKE A PORTION OF A PLEADING MUST QUOTE IN FULL THE PORTIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN EXCEPT WHERE THE MOTION IS TO STRIKE AN ENTIRE PARAGRAPH CAUSE OF ACTION, COUNT OR DEFENSE. SPECIFICATIONS IN A NOTICE MUST BE NUMBERED CONSECUTIVELY.") ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976.(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. AND KEVIN WALLACE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") IS OVERRULED ON ALL GROUNDS. AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3 AND 6, THE FAC STATES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS; EACH ADEQUATELY PLEADS THE STATUTORY ELEMENTS OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. EACH ALSO ADEQUATELY PLEADS WHO, WHAT, WHEN, AND HOW THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE. (CCP 430.10(E).) THE DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION ON THE GROUND THAT IT IS DEFECTIVE AS TO FORM IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED; IT ALLEGES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE. THE DEMURRERS ON GROUND OF UNCERTAINTY ARE OVERRULED. (CCP 430.10(F).) AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-4 AND 6, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ADDRESSED IN THEIR MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES HOW THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE UNCERTAIN. THE TITLE OF THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT MAKE THAT CAUSE OF ACTION UNCERTAIN AND THE DEMURRER THAT IT FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALSO OVERRULED. THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION; A DEMURRER WILL NOT LIE TO ATTACK A CLAIM FOR A DAMAGE AMOUNT REQUESTED IN A COMPLAINT. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6(C) ("IF A MOTION CHALLENGES THE SUFFICIENCY OF A PLEADING'MOVING PARTY MUST ALSO SUPPLY A COURTESY COPY OF THAT PLEADING"). THE COURT DID NOT DENY THE MOTION ON THIS GROUND. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976. (302/MJM)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-12-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL, ESQ. of Law Offices of Michael Brooks Carroll, Tel No. 415-788-7600 appearing for Plaintiffs;
Atty. LORENA MATEI, ESQ. of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara LLP, Tel No. 510-540-4881 appearing for Defendants Wallace Remodeling, Inc. and Kevin Wallace.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. AND KEVIN WALLACE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is granted as to the sixth and seventh causes of action only and only as to defendant Kevin Wallace and denied in all other respects. Because Mr. Wallace is not a party to the contract, he has no liability on the two contract claims. Plaintiff's admissible evidence has raised triable issues on all other claims against Mr. Wallace and all claims against WRI. The triable issues include whether: 1) Mr. Wallace made material misrepresentations to plaintiff about the work and services of WRI; 2) any of the work performed by WRI fell below applicable trade standards 3) any breaches or nonperformance by WRI were substantial; and 4) WRI and Mr. Wallace engaged in deceptive billing practices, impermissible front end loading, and crossed out required contract language. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-25-2015 Set For Trial On Nov-09-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Continued To Mar-11-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. and KEVIN WALLACE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted without leave to amend in part. The motion is granted as to paragraph 42 of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), as punitive and exemplary damages are not recoverable for violations of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200. (Czechowski v. Tandy Corp. (N.D. Cal. 1990) 731 F.Supp.406, 410, "plaintiffs' claim under section 17200 of the Business and Professions code will not support an award of punitive damages either;" see also Stern, Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 Practice, par. 8:104 (The Rutter Group 2013).) The rest of the motion to strike is denied in its entirety. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, TEL. NO. (415)717-9768 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-17-2014 Continued To Jan-28-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2014 Continued To Dec-03-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Park Jennings, Esq. of the Law Offices of Michael B. Carroll, T:415.788.7600, for plaintiff and prevailing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. and KEVIN WALLACE's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied without prejudice. Moving party failed to comply with Rule of Court 3.1322 ("A notice of motion to strike a portion of a pleading must quote in full the portions sought to be stricken except where the motion is to strike an entire paragraph cause of action, count or defense. Specifications in a notice must be numbered consecutively.") ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Park Jennings, Esq. of the Law Offices of Michael B. Carroll, T:415.788.7600, for plaintiff and prevailing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS WALLACE REMODELING, INC. and KEVIN WALLACE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") is overruled on all grounds. As to causes of action 1-3 and 6, the FAC states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against Defendants; each adequately pleads the statutory elements of these causes of action. Each also adequately pleads who, what, when, and how the alleged misrepresentations were made. (CCP 430.10(e).) The demurrer to the 3rd cause of action on the ground that it is defective as to form is overruled. The demurrer to the 4th cause of action is overruled; it alleges facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for professional negligence. The demurrers on ground of uncertainty are overruled. (CCP 430.10(f).) As to causes of action 1-4 and 6, Defendants have not addressed in their memorandum of points and authorities how these causes of action are uncertain. The title of the 5th cause of action does not make that cause of action uncertain and the demurrer that it fails to state a cause of action is also overruled. The 6th cause of action states a cause of action; a demurrer will not lie to attack a claim for a damage amount requested in a complaint. The Court notes that the motion does not comply with Local Rule 2.6(c) ("if a motion challenges the sufficiency of a pleading'moving party must also supply a courtesy copy of that pleading"). The Court did not deny the motion on this ground. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
